posted_snapshot_policy: standards/posted contains the current published revision of every active draft-schrock-* Datatracker record. XML and TXT snapshots were checked against the IETF archive on 2026-07-21.

status_language.active_individual_submission: Published on the IETF Datatracker as an individual Internet-Draft. Not an RFC, not an adopted working-group item, and not IETF endorsement.
status_language.candidate: Repository source under review. Not filed.
status_language.next_wave: Assigned to a dated filing wave but still subject to its explicit technical, related-work, and claim-tracing gates.
status_language.retired_absorbed: Standalone draft is not a filing candidate. Useful substance has an identified canonical owner.
status_language.partner_triggered_profile: Application or commercial profile that returns to the filing lane only with a named external validating party.
status_language.held: Temporary state only. No document may remain held without a dated decision or explicit external trigger.

decision_vocabulary.VERIFIED: One artifact passed its native verifier under relying-party-selected trust inputs.
decision_vocabulary.MATCH: Independently verified artifacts denote the same material action directly or under exact pinned mapping profiles.
decision_vocabulary.SATISFIED: Verified and matched evidence fills every slot in the relying party's evidence requirement.
decision_vocabulary.AUTHORIZED: The relying party's local policy permits execution.
decision_vocabulary.EXECUTED: An executor asserts or attests that an effect occurred.

portfolio_layers.name: identity_transport
portfolio_layers.question: Who or what is present in the live channel?
portfolio_layers.emilia_position: Consume and bind; do not redefine WIMSE, SPIFFE, or native agent identity.

portfolio_layers.name: delegation_capability_policy
portfolio_layers.question: What authority, capability, or machine policy applies?
portfolio_layers.emilia_position: Verify under native rules and treat policy ALLOW as distinct from human approval.

portfolio_layers.name: material_action_identity_mapping
portfolio_layers.question: Do different native artifacts denote the same material action?
portfolio_layers.emilia_position: CAID plus relying-party-pinned Action-Mapping Profiles. This is the first post-blackout land claim.

portfolio_layers.name: authorization_evidence
portfolio_layers.question: What approval or authorization event occurred under a named profile?
portfolio_layers.emilia_position: Receipts, human-authorization binding, and quorum as non-exclusive evidence profiles.

portfolio_layers.name: evidence_satisfaction
portfolio_layers.question: Does the verified, matched bundle fill the relying party's required evidence slots?
portfolio_layers.emilia_position: AEC returns SATISFIED or UNSATISFIED, never a universal authorization verdict.

portfolio_layers.name: execution_lifecycle
portfolio_layers.question: Was evidence demanded, consumed, executed, observed, revoked, and preserved correctly?
portfolio_layers.emilia_position: Challenge, enforcement, outcome, revocation, evidence record, and application profiles remain separate transitions.

july_19_2026_core_wave.verification: Each posted TXT is byte-for-byte identical to the corresponding https://www.ietf.org/archive/id/ artifact.

items.claim: Typed material action identity plus profile-bounded cross-format matching
Cross-domain evidence architecture and non-collapsing decision vocabulary
Trust-root introduction and scoped human authority
One explicit action-bound approval evidence profile with scoped replay and offline guarantees
Ordered multi-party ceremony and distinct-human policy semantics
Action-bound capability budget and spend evidence
Relying-party evidence satisfaction over natively verified, action-matched components
Executor-side authorization-evidence clearance for model-directed physical execution
items.limitations: Reference implementation and synthetic vectors only; no wet-lab deployment, screening capability, scientific-safety determination, physical-truth claim, or external endorsement

retired_absorbed.canonical_owner: Proof predicates in relying-party profiles and native receipt verifier outputs
retired_absorbed.reason: S/H/V/Q created a second assurance language beside deployed receipt and authority vocabularies. The standalone taxonomy is retired before filing.
The active Authority Introduction draft owns trust-root introduction and scoped authority.
Its composition map is fully absorbed by Architecture-01.
Crypto agility, re-anchoring, and hybrid anti-stripping belong with long-term evidence preservation rather than a standalone algorithm catalog.

partner_triggered_profiles.trigger: A regulator, government program, or management-system standards partner validates the control-to-evidence mapping.
A bank, insurer, underwriter, or counterparty requires interoperable reliance terms and participates in legal review.

consolidation_rules: Architecture-01 absorbs the staged Agent Trust Stack map.
AEC owns component verification, action binding, and evidence satisfaction; it does not define effect or ceremony records.
Quorum owns ordered multi-handshake and distinct-human policy semantics.
Receipts owns the base receipt and explicit approval profile, not every approval mechanism.
Challenge will profile AuthZEN AARP's requestable denial and PDP-verifiable approval state instead of redefining its workflow.
Revocation owns terminal negative evidence; current non-revocation is a separately authenticated, policy-fresh status input and may profile Token Status List.
Assurance requirements are verifier-visible proof predicates; no standalone universal S/H/V/Q taxonomy is being filed.
Authority Introduction owns trust-root introduction and scoped authority; no separate Authority Registry draft is filed.
Evidence Record owns crypto agility and hybrid anti-stripping; no standalone PQC algorithm catalog is filed.

matching_claim.name: CAID Action-Mapping Profile
matching_claim.safe_novelty_language: The authors are not aware of an existing specification that combines a typed material-action registry with a relying-party-pinned, loss-aware cross-format projection and the closed verdicts EQUIVALENT_UNDER_PROFILE, NOT_EQUIVALENT, and INDETERMINATE.
not_claimed: Correctness of a relying party's selected mapping profile
Exclusive ownership of action digests or portable approval evidence

discovery_index.use: Discovery and hypothesis generation only; not a source-locked basis for public claims.

source_locked_observatory.use: Revision-pinned public comparisons and related-work claims.

acknowledgment_basis.Eric Rescorla: Prompted separation of baseline verifier hygiene from the concrete cross-domain validation requirement.
acknowledgment_basis.Linda Dunbar: Her individual Agent Gateway gap-analysis draft provided the cross-operator and cross-administrative-domain use case; this does not imply DMSC BoF consensus.
acknowledgment_basis.Chris Hood: Agent transport and composition work sharpened the boundary between carrying context and independently verifying evidence.
